Robles Tzunun v. Mukasey | (2009-01-20) | 9th Cir. | Lead Opinion

MEMORANDUM **

Juan Paulino Robles Tzunun, a native and citizen of Guatemala, petitions for review of the Board of Immigration Appeals’ (“BIA”) order dismissing his appeal from an immigration judge’s order denying his application for cancellation of removal. We have jurisdiction pursuant to 8 U.S.C. § 1252. We review de novo claims of constitutional violations in immigration proceedings. Ram v. INS, 243 F.3d 510, 516 (9th Cir.200

United States v. Alfred Darnell Ford | (2004-06-08) | 9th Cir. | Per Curiam

O’SCANNLAIN, Circuit Judge:
 

 We must decide whether the United States is barred by double jeopardy or collateral estoppel from prosecuting an owner for “managing and controlling” real estate for the purpose of distributing cocaine when he was previously acquitted of “knowingly opening” the same place for such purpose.

PER CURIAM:

Upon rehearing, we contemplate the extent to which 28 U.S.C. § 2410 waives the United States’ sovereign immunity from suit in tax cases. Our previous unpublished memorandum, 978 F.2d 715, filed October 23, 1992, is withdrawn.,

United States v. Alfred Darnell Ford | (2004-06-08) | 9th Cir. | Per Curiam

 The Government counters Ford’s claim of double jeopardy by arguing that the
 
 Blockburger
 
 test is met here. The
 
 Block-burger
 
 test asks “whether each offense contains an element not contained in the other; if not, they are the ‘same offense’ and double jeopardy bars additional punishment and successive prosecution.”
 
 United States v. Dixon,
 
 509 U.S. 688, 696, 113 S.Ct. 2849, 125 L.Ed.2d 556 (1993);
